tools.name: reminders_create
tools.description: Schedule a reminder. One-shot by default; pass `recurrence` (coarse label) OR `recurrence_spec` (exact cron cadence) to make it repeat. Returns the new reminder's id and the persisted fire_at (unix seconds).

message.description: Reminder body — composed verbatim or wrapped by the fire-time agent.

fire_at.description: Unix seconds (UTC) when the reminder should fire (the FIRST occurrence when recurring).

project_id.description: Optional project scope; persisted as topic_id under the hood.

recurrence.description: Optional COARSE cadence — omit for a one-shot. weekly = every 7 days from fire_at, monthly ≈ every 30 days, occasional ≈ every 14 days. For exact wall-clock cadences (daily at 9am, weekdays, annually) use `recurrence_spec` instead. Mutually exclusive with recurrence_spec.

recurrence_spec.description: Optional FAITHFUL cron cadence — a standard 5-field cron expression (minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week). Examples: '0 9 * * *' = daily 09:00, '0 9 * * 1-5' = weekdays 09:00, '0 9 7 2 *' = annually on Feb 7, '0 */6 * * *' = every 6 hours. Wall-clock and DST-correct. Mutually exclusive with recurrence.

tools.name: reminders_list
tools.description: List pending reminders for this owner, sorted by fire_at ASCENDING (soonest-firing first). Optional project_id narrows to a single project. v1 only lists pending reminders — fired/cancelled history is not yet exposed; the status field is reserved for forward-compat and may only be set to 'pending'.

limit.description: Maximum number of reminders to return. Backend default applies when omitted.

project_id.description: Optional project scope; matched against the row's topic_id.

status.description: v1 only supports 'pending'. fired/cancelled history is not yet exposed; the field stays for forward-compat.

tools.name: reminders_snooze
tools.description: Move a pending reminder to a later fire_at. Implemented as atomic cancel + re-create against the underlying engine so the engine's public API stays untouched; returns the NEW reminder id (the original id is cancelled).

new_fire_at.description: Unix seconds (UTC) for the new fire time.

id.description: Id of the newly-created reminder carrying the new fire_at.

cancelled_id.description: Id of the original reminder that was cancelled.

tools.name: reminders_cancel
tools.description: Cancel a pending reminder. Returns ok=true when a pending row was cancelled, ok=false when the row was already fired/cancelled or never existed. Returns ok=false for both unknown ids and reminders owned by another owner (info-hiding is intentional — the caller is never told a foreign id exists).

tools.name: reminders_convert_to_task
tools.description: Promote a pending reminder into a canonical task. Creates a task with the reminder's message as the title and the reminder's fire_at as the due_date; the task substrate then auto-creates a fresh linked reminder. The original reminder is cancelled so it does not fire twice. Returns the new task_id, the linked_reminder_id (the freshly-created reminder bound to the task), and the cancelled_reminder_id (the original).

project_id.description: Optional project scope for the new task. Defaults to the reminder's project.

title.description: Optional title override; falls back to the reminder's message.

tools.name: reminders_update
tools.description: Rewrite a pending reminder's message body. Implemented as atomic cancel+create inside projectDb.transaction so the engine surface stays untouched and the audit trail records both writes. Preserves the original row's topic_id + fire_at + recurrence + source (NULL for organic, '@neutronai/reminders-core' for Core-owned). Returns the new id, the replaced id, and the persisted new message body. Throws when id is unknown / belongs to another owner (info-hidden as 'not found') / is not pending.

message.description: New message body. Replaces the original verbatim.

id.description: Id of the new reminder carrying the new message.

replaced_id.description: Id of the original reminder that was cancelled.

message.description: The new message body (echoed for confirmation).

tools.name: rituals_propose
tools.description: PROPOSE a ritual: a recurring, UNATTENDED sub-agent that runs a fixed prompt on a schedule (e.g. a morning brief that reads your STATUS.md + calendar). IMPORTANT: proposing does NOT schedule or run anything — the ritual only ever fires after the OWNER explicitly APPROVES it in chat (a code-rendered approval message with Approve/Deny buttons is shown; approval is content-hash-bound, so any later edit to the prompt/surface/cadence requires re-approval). Returns an opaque proposal_id and status 'pending_approval'. Read-only tool surfaces (Read/Glob/Grep) and web (WebSearch/WebFetch, which also needs a separate egress approval) are supported; write/exec tools (Bash/Write/Edit) are currently blocked at fire time.

id.description: Charset-guarded ritual id (lowercase alphanumeric + hyphen, 1-64 chars, starts alnum). Also the prompt file basename. Must not already exist.

description.description: One-line human capability summary rendered in the approval prompt (non-empty, <= 200 chars).

tool_surface.description: Built-in tool names the ritual may use at fire time (e.g. ['Read','Glob','Grep'] or ['Read','WebSearch']). Never empty. Web tools require egress:'web'. Write/exec tools are refused at fire time until OS sandboxing ships.

egress.description: 'none' = no internet; 'web' = the ritual may reach the public internet (WebSearch/WebFetch) — a SEPARATE owner approval is required for this.

silent.description: When true, no completion message is posted after a successful run. Defaults false.

prompt.description: The FULL prompt the ritual runs verbatim each fire. NFC-normalized; control/bidi/zero-width characters are rejected; capped at 16 KiB (refused, never truncated). Shown in full inside the approval message.

schedule.description: When the ritual runs. fire_at is the first occurrence (unix seconds). Pass at most one cadence; omit both for a one-shot.

fire_at.description: Unix seconds (UTC) of the FIRST occurrence.

recurrence.description: Optional coarse cadence. Mutually exclusive with recurrence_spec.

recurrence_spec.description: Optional 5-field cron cadence (e.g. '0 9 * * *'). Mutually exclusive with recurrence.

proposal_id.description: Opaque handle for the pending proposal.

status.description: Always 'pending_approval' — the ritual will not fire until the owner approves it.

requires_egress_approval.description: True for egress:'web' defs — a SECOND owner approval (network egress) is required before scheduling.

tools.name: rituals_enable
tools.description: ENABLE an already-registered ritual by giving it a schedule. Use this for the BUNDLED example rituals (morning-brief, evening-wrap, daily-delta) and any previously-registered ritual — rituals_propose refuses their ids because their prompt already exists on disk. You supply ONLY the id + schedule; the prompt, tool surface and scope are owned by the registered def (see rituals_status for the ids). IMPORTANT: enabling does NOT run anything — like rituals_propose, the ritual only fires after the OWNER explicitly APPROVES it in chat (a code-rendered Approve/Deny prompt is shown; approval is content-hash-bound to the on-disk prompt bytes). Returns an opaque proposal_id and status 'pending_approval'.

id.description: The id of an already-registered ritual (from rituals_status) — e.g. 'morning-brief'. Must NOT already be enabled.

schedule.description: When the ritual runs. fire_at is the first occurrence (unix seconds). Pass at most one cadence; omit both for a one-shot.

fire_at.description: Unix seconds (UTC) of the FIRST occurrence.

recurrence.description: Optional coarse cadence. Mutually exclusive with recurrence_spec.

recurrence_spec.description: Optional 5-field cron cadence (e.g. '0 9 * * *'). Mutually exclusive with recurrence.

proposal_id.description: Opaque handle for the pending proposal.

status.description: Always 'pending_approval' — the ritual will not fire until the owner approves it.

requires_egress_approval.description: True for egress:'web' defs — a SECOND owner approval (network egress) is required before scheduling.

tools.name: rituals_status
tools.description: List every registered ritual with its approval state ('approved' | 'pending' | 'denied' | 'none') and whether it is currently scheduled. Read-only. Use this to see which proposed rituals the owner has approved.
